methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Canada Rocket Company is a Seed-stage space and aerospace venture founded by Hugh Kolias and David Tenny, having raised $6.2M to date. The broader Space & Aerospace sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct peer valuation data at the Seed stage is limited.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method is reliably applicable. Using total capital raised ($6.2M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $15.5M. The Peer-Median approach cannot be applied—while 23 same-sector peers exist, insufficient data points meet narrow Seed-stage or adjacent-tier filters. Public-Comp Anchoring is similarly inapplicable; the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 49.8x median P/S across public names, but the target's revenue is not disclosed, preventing meaningful application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $18M, centered at $16M, derived solely from stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data for public-comp extrapolation, limited private Seed-stage comparables in space technology, and the early-stage nature of the business, which restricts reliance on financial metrics. A fuller valuation would require revenue disclosure or access to broader Seed-stage transaction data in aerospace.
